practiceScenarios.scenario: A caller is distressed and speaking very quickly. You can hear another person shouting in the background, but you have not yet confirmed the exact location.
options: Ask the caller to calm down and explain everything from the beginning.
Politely interrupt to confirm the exact location and whether anyone is in immediate danger.
Transfer the call to a supervisor because the caller is difficult to understand.
Stay silent and wait for the caller to finish before asking any questions.

practiceScenarios.scenario: A caller reports a minor road collision. During the call, they mention one driver is becoming aggressive and trying to leave the scene.
options: Tell the caller to physically stop the driver leaving.
Focus only on exchanging insurance details because the collision is minor.
Record the developing risk, keep the caller safe, and gather vehicle and direction details.
End the call and wait for an online report because there are no injuries.

practiceScenarios.scenario: A caller says their elderly neighbour has not been seen for two days. They can hear a faint noise from inside the property but cannot get a response.
options: Advise the caller to wait another day and call back if nothing changes.
Ask the caller to force entry immediately.
Treat it as a welfare concern, confirm location/access details, and arrange an appropriate response.
Log it as information only because the caller has not seen an injury.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports hearing screaming from a neighbouring flat. They do not know the flat number but believe the sound is coming from the floor above.
options: Ask the caller to investigate the corridor and identify the exact flat.
Gather the caller's location, what they can hear, any known risks, and arrange a response while keeping them safe.
Tell the caller no action can be taken without a flat number.
Advise the caller to record the noise and submit it later.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller says a person is lying on the pavement after a fight outside a pub. The caller is across the road and does not know if the person is conscious.
options: Ask the caller to approach the injured person immediately.
Confirm the exact location, assess immediate danger from a safe position, and request relevant emergency support.
Tell the caller to wait until the fight has fully ended before providing details.
Record the incident as public disorder only because injury is unconfirmed.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports a vehicle driving erratically on a busy road. They are following the vehicle closely to keep it in sight.
options: Encourage the caller to continue following so the vehicle is not lost.
Tell the caller to overtake and read the registration plate.
Advise the caller to keep themselves safe, gather location/direction details, and avoid following dangerously.
Ask the caller to call back only if a collision happens.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller is reporting threatening messages from an ex-partner. They say the ex-partner has just sent a message saying they are outside.
options: Treat it as a current risk, confirm safety/location, gather details, and arrange an appropriate response.
Tell the caller to block the number and call back tomorrow.
Ask the caller to go outside and confirm whether the ex-partner is there.
Log the messages as a non-urgent report because no assault has happened.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports smoke coming from a small industrial unit. They are unsure if anyone is inside, and staff from nearby units are gathering outside.
options: Ask staff nearby to enter and check whether anyone is inside.
Confirm location, visible hazards, possible occupants, and advise people to stay clear while response is arranged.
Tell the caller to wait until flames are visible before escalating.
Record it as an alarm activation only because the caller is outside.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller whispers that someone has broken into their home. They are upstairs with the door locked and can hear movement below.
options: Ask the caller to go downstairs and check who is there.
Keep the caller on the line, confirm location and safety, gather what can be heard, and arrange urgent support.
Tell the caller to shout loudly so the intruder leaves.
Advise the caller to hang up and hide until morning.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports a child separated from their parent in a crowded shopping centre. Security staff are searching but the child has not been found.
options: Advise the caller to wait because shopping centres often reunite children quickly.
Gather description, last known location, time missing, vulnerabilities, and coordinate an appropriate response.
Tell the parent to search outside alone.
Record it as lost property because the child is inside a managed venue.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports a person sitting on the wrong side of a bridge barrier. Traffic is still moving below.
options: Ask the caller to pull the person back over the barrier.
Keep the caller safe, confirm exact location, assess immediate risk, and arrange urgent support.
Tell the caller to leave because it is not their responsibility.
Ask the caller to take a photo so responders can identify the person.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller reports a shop theft. The suspect has left, but staff have CCTV and the suspect's direction of travel.
options: Gather key details, preserve evidence, record direction of travel, and assess whether immediate attendance is needed.
Tell staff to chase the suspect while staying on the phone.
Ignore the direction of travel because the suspect has already left.
Advise staff to delete CCTV after writing a description.

finalScenarios.scenario: A caller says their friend has taken tablets and is becoming drowsy. The caller is panicking and does not know the address because they are visiting.
options: Ask the caller to search their phone for the address before doing anything else.
Establish the location using all available clues, assess breathing/consciousness, and arrange urgent medical help.
Tell the caller to drive the friend to hospital immediately.
End the call and wait for the caller to find the exact postcode.
